title: A circuit arrangement and a method for operating a circuit arrangement
abstract: A circuit arrangement comprises a first input node (11), a first output node (21), a sampling capacitor means (Cs, Cs1, Cs2) and a first switching means being switchable between a first switching state and a second switching state. The first switching means is coupled to the sampling capacitor means (Cs, Cs1, Cs2), the first input node (11) and the first output node (21) in such a way that the sampling capacitor means (Cs, Cs1, Cs2) is conductively connected to the first input node (11) and disconnected from the first output node (21) in the first switching state and the sampling capacitor means (Cs, Cs1, Cs2) is disconnected from the first input node (11) and conductively connected to the first output node (21) in the second switching state. A first charge-storing element is coupled via a second switching means to the first input node (11) in such a way that the charge-storing element is charged in the first switching state and discharged in the second switching state, thereby at least partly compensating current flow for charging the sampling capacitor means (Cs, Cs1, Cs2) in the first switching state.
claims:
Claims Claims 1. A circuit arrangement comprising - a first input node (11); - a first output node (21); - a sampling capacitor means (Cs, Csl, Cs2); - a first switching means being switchable between a first switching state and a second switching state, the first switching means being coupled to the sampling capacitor means (Cs, Csl, Cs2), the first input node (11) and the first output node (21) in such a way that the sampling capacitor means (Cs, Csl, Cs2) is conductively connected to the first input node (11) and disconnected from the first output node (21) in the first switching state and the sampling capacitor means (Cs, Csl, Cs2) is disconnected from the first input node (11) and conductively connected to the first output node (21) in the second switching state; - a first charge-storing element being coupled via a second switching means to the first input node (11) in such a way that the charge-storing element is charged in the first switching state and discharged in the second switching state, thereby at least partly compensating current flow for charging the sampling capacitor means (Cs, Csl, Cs2) in the first switching state. 2. The circuit arrangement according to claim 1, further comprising - a second input node (12); - a second output node (22); - a second charge-storing element being coupled via the second switching means to the second input node (12) in such a way that the second charge-storing element is charged in 

the first switching state and discharged in the second switching state; - wherein the first switching means is coupled to the second input node (12) and the second output node (22) in such a way that the sampling capacitor means (Cs, Csl, Cs2) is conductively connected to the second input node (12) and disconnected from the second output node (22) in the first switching state and the sampling capacitor means (Cs, Csl, Cs2) is disconnected from the second input node (12) and conductively connected to the second output node (22) in the second switching state, thereby at least partly compensating current flow for charging the sampling capacitor means (Cs, Csl, Cs2) in the first switching state. 3. The circuit arrangement according to claim 1 or 2, wherein the first charge-storing element comprising a first capacitor (CI) is charged via a first buffer amplifier means (31) and/or the second charge-storing element comprising a second capacitor (C2) is charged via a second buffer amplifier means (32). 4. The circuit arrangement according to claim 3, wherein the capacitances of the first and second capacitors (CI, C2) are equal or nearly equal. 5. The circuit arrangement according to claim 3 or 4, wherein the first and/or second buffer amplifier means (31, 32) comprise chopping means (315, 316, 325, 326) . 6. The circuit arrangement according to any of claims 3 to 5, wherein the first buffer amplifier means (31) has an input (311) that is coupled to the first input node (11) and an output (312), and the second buffer amplifier means (32) has 

an input (321) that is coupled to the second input node (12) and an output (322); the first capacitor (CI) being conductively coupled to the outputs (312, 322) of the first and second buffer amplifier means (31, 32) in the first switching state and at least one terminal of the first capacitor (CI) being disconnected from the outputs (312, 322) of the first and second buffer amplifier means (31,32) in the second switching state; the second capacitor (C2) being conductively coupled to the outputs (312, 322) of the first and second buffer amplifier means (31, 32) in the first switching state and at least one terminal of the second capacitor (C2) being disconnected from the outputs (312, 322) of the first and second buffer amplifier means (31,32) in the second switching state; the first and second capacitors (CI, C2) being conductively connected in parallel in the first switching state. 7. The circuit arrangement according to any of claims 3 to 6, wherein a first circuit branch comprises the first buffer amplifier means (31), a downstream first switch (SOI) and second switch (S02) downstream of the first switch (SOI); the input (311) of the first buffer amplifier means (31) and the second switch (S02) being coupled to the first input node (11) . 8. The circuit arrangement according to any of claims 3 to 7, wherein a second circuit branch comprises the second buffer amplifier means (32), a downstream third switch (S03) and a fourth switch (S04) downstream of the third switch (S03) ; the input (321) of the second buffer amplifier means (32) and the fourth switch (S04) being coupled to the second input node (12) . 9. The circuit arrangement according to claim 8, wherein a fifth switch (S05) and a downstream sixth switch (S06) are coupled between the outputs (312, 322) of the first and second buffer amplifier means (31, 32); and wherein a seventh switch (S07) and a downstream eighth switch (S08) are coupled between the outputs (312, 322) of the first and second buffer amplifier means (31, 32) . 10. The circuit arrangement according to claim 9, wherein one terminal of the first capacitor (CI) is arranged between the first and second switches (SOI, S02); the other terminal of first capacitor (CI) being arranged between the fifth and sixth switches (S05, S06); and wherein one terminal of the second capacitor (C2) is arranged between the third and fourth switches (S03, S04); the other terminal of second capacitor (C2) being arranged between the seventh and eighth switches (S07, S08) . 11. The circuit arrangement according to any of claims 2 to 10, wherein the first switching means comprises a first sampling switch (SI) and a second sampling switch (S2) that are coupled between the first input and output nodes (11, 21) and a third sampling switch (S3) and a fourth sampling switch (S4) that are coupled between the second input and output nodes (21, 22) . 12. The circuit arrangement according to claim 11, further comprising - third switching means (5) having a first pair of terminals (Cint, Cinb) , a second pair of terminals (Clt, Clb) and a third pair of terminals (C2t, C2b) and being coupled between the first and second switching means and the capacitive means 

 (C) containing the first capacitor (CI), the second capacitor (C2) and the sampling capacitor means (Cs, Csl, Cs2); - a control means (4) for controlling the third switching means (5) in such a way that one of the capacitive means (C) is conductively coupled between the first pair of terminals (Cint, Cinb) , another one of the capacitive means (C) is conductively coupled between the second pair of terminals (Clt, Clb) and another one of the capacitive means (C) is conductively coupled between the third pair of terminals (C2t, C2b) ; thereby coupling the capacitive means (C) to the pairs of terminals (Cint, Cinb, Clt, Clb, C2t, C2b) in a given coupling order; the control means (4) being suitable for changing the coupling order. 13. An analog-to-digital converter comprising the circuit arrangement according to any of the claims 1 to 12. 14. A measurement device comprising the circuit arrangement according to any of the claims 1 to 12. 15. A method for operating a circuit arrangement comprising a sampling capacitor means (Cs, Csl, Cs2) that is conductively connected to a first input node (11) and disconnected from a first output node (12) in a first switching state and that is disconnected from the first input node (11) and conductively connected to the first output node (12) in a second switching state; the method comprising: charging a first charge-storing element in the first switching state and discharging it in the second switching state in such a manner that a first compensation current flows to the first input node (11) in the second switching state . 16. The method according to claim 15, wherein the sampling capacitor means (Cs, Csl, Cs2) is conductively connected to a second input node (12) and disconnected from a second output node (22) in a first switching state and the sampling capacitor means (Cs, Csl, Cs2) is disconnected from the second input node (12) and conductively connected to the second output node (22) in a second switching state; the method further comprising: charging a second energy-storing element in the first switching state and discharging it in the second switching state in such a manner that a second compensation current flows to the second input node (12) in the second switching state . 17. The method according to claim 15 or 16, wherein the first charge-storing element is charged via a first buffer amplifier means (31); the first compensation current being suitable for at least partly compensating a current having charged the sampling capacitor means (Cs, Csl, Cs2) in the first switching state; and/or wherein the second charge-storing element is charged via a second buffer amplifier means (32); the second compensation current being suitable for at least partly compensating a current having charged the sampling capacitor means (Cs, Csl, Cs2) in the first switching state.
